channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Multimodal deep learning for inflammatory bowel disease: a new frontier in cellular and molecular biomarker discovery to clinical translation.
item.description: Journal of biological engineering - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), including Crohn’s disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is a chronic, relapsing condition with heterogeneous clinical phenotypes and variable therapeutic outco...

item.title: Artificial intelligence in inflammatory bowel disease: bridging innovation, implementation and impact.
item.description: Nature reviews. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - Artificial intelligence (AI) is rapidly transforming the management landscape of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). While early applications in endoscopy, digital pathology and cross-sectional imagi...

item.title: Chat-based Artificial Intelligence model as a source of information for Inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Arab journal of gastroenterology : the official publication of the Pan-Arab Association of Gastroenterology - Artificial intelligence models could be a promising tool for providing scientific information to patients. The aim was to study the accuracy of the responses provided by Chat GPT (Generative Pre-tr...

item.title: Klebsiella Enrichment Is Associated with Disease Severity in Ulcerative Colitis.
item.description: Journal of applied microbiology - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a chronic inflammatory bowel disease. This study uniquely undertook a parallel, severity-stratified comparison of both fecal and mucosal microbiota and metabolites in UC pa...

item.title: The roles and heterogeneity of CD8+ T cells in inflammatory bowel disease: A narrative review of insights from single‑cell transcriptomics (Review).
item.description: International journal of molecular medicine - Review - The present review investigates the role and characteristics of CD8+ T cells in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) using single‑cell transcriptomics, revealing their pivotal functions and remarkable ...

item.title: Exploring the mechanism of Qinlian Yuyang Decoction in the treatment of ulcerative colitis based on multi-omics technology.
item.description: Chinese medicine - The global incidence of ulcerative colitis (UC) is increasing, yet effective clinical treatment options remain limited. Qinlian Yuyang Decoction (QYD), a modified formulation based on Gegen Qinlian...

item.title: Navigating the Diagnostic Continuum: Feline Lymphoplasmacytic Enteritis and Low-Grade Intestinal T-cell Lymphoma.
item.description: The Veterinary clinics of North America. Small animal practice - Review - This article discusses the importance of accurately diagnosing feline gastrointestinal diseases, particularly differentiating inflammatory bowel disease from alimentary lymphoma. It emphasizes that...

item.title: Advances in the use of Janus kinase inhibitors.
item.description: Current opinion in rheumatology - Over the last two decades, oral small molecules that target intracellular protein kinases that mediate intracellular signaling have been found to be effective in multiple immune mediated inflammato...

item.title: Beyond drug delivery: nanoparticles as active modulators of immunometabolism for treating inflammatory diseases.
item.description: Journal of drug targeting - Review - Immunometabolism is central to chronic inflammatory diseases, with metabolic reprogramming including dysregulated glycolysis, mitochondrial dysfunction, and excessive ROS production driving patholo...

item.title: Comparative pathogenicity of very virulent and novel variant infectious bursal disease virus Egyptian strains in chickens.
item.description: Virology journal - Infectious bursal disease (IBD) is an important viral disease that causing severe immunosuppression in young chickens. This current study compared the pathogenicity of very virulent IBDV (vvIBDV) a...

item.title: INTELCAPE: A Deep Learning-Powered System for Automated, High-Accuracy Crohn's Disease Diagnosis via Capsule Endoscopy.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Capsule endoscopy (CE) is a non-invasive technique for diagnosing Crohn's disease (CD); however, manual interpretation of CE videos is time-consuming and error-prone. We developed an artificial int...

item.title: Inflammatory bowel disease and extracellular matrix: when victim becomes double agent.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - Review - The extracellular matrix (ECM) represents an intricate network of proteins present in all organs, with specific physical and biochemical functions. ECM is composed of two distinct but connected ent...

item.title: Letter: Incremental Value and Outcome Modelling in Frailty Assessment for Older Patients With Inflammatory Bowel Disease.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Letter -

item.title: Disparities in Access to Gastrointestinal Care in the United States.
item.description: Digestive diseases and sciences - Review - Disparities in gastrointestinal (GI) care in the United States represent a major health burden, with GI diseases accounting for approximately $135 billion annually in direct medical expenditures. A...

item.title: Ulcerative colitis, frailty, and beyond.
item.description: Expert review of gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - Frailty is a geriatric construct that is increasingly pertinent to patients with ulcerative colitis (UC). Understanding how frailty and UC interact will be imperative to incorporating frailty into ...

item.title: Microscopic colitis and primary sclerosing cholangitis are bidirectionally associated: A nationwide matched cohort and case-control study.
item.description: Journal of internal medicine - Microscopic colitis (MC) is an inflammatory disease of the colon. Although there is a known association between inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC), evidence l...

item.title: Conjunctival ulcer in Crohn's disease: a complete slit-lamp photography follow-up.
item.description: Eye (London, England) -

item.title: Identifying key predictors of metastatic cutaneous Crohn's disease: a case-control study.
item.description: The American journal of gastroenterology - Metastatic cutaneous Crohn's Disease (MCD) is a rare cutaneous manifestation of Crohn's Disease with significant morbidity, yet understanding is limited to case reports and small series. We aimed t...

item.title: Neuro-immune interactions underlying autoimmune diseases: insights from brain imaging data.
item.description: Molecular brain - Autoimmune diseases (AIDs) result from intricate interactions among genetic, environmental and immune factors, with emerging evidence underscoring the role of the central nervous system (CNS) in th...

item.title: Enhancing Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) competency in gastroenterology fellowship through traditional, digital, and experiential learning pathways.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - The rapidly evolving field of Inflammatory Bowel Disease (IBD) places growing educational demands on gastroenterology trainees, many of whom report gaps in confidence and preparedness in managing I...
